hero.title: 3 Days in Kolkata.
hero.titleAccent: The City of Joy, Trams and Grand Old Bengal.
hero.intro: Kolkata moves to its own beat. Colonial grandeur fading gracefully, yellow taxis and trams, intellectual cafes, and a food culture all its own. The old capital of British India still has more soul than almost anywhere.

overview.heading: 3 Days Kolkata Heritage & Culture Tour: The Full Picture
body: Kolkata, the capital of West Bengal and once the seat of British India, is one of the country's most characterful cities. The grand Victoria Memorial, the iron span of Howrah Bridge, the riverside temples and the legendary intellectual and artistic life give it a depth few Indian cities match.

This 3 day Kolkata heritage package from Malaysia is private throughout. Your vehicle and driver guide take you through the colonial core, the river ghats, the flower market and the sweet shops that Bengalis are rightly proud of, all at your own pace.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Victoria Memorial & Colonial Core
items.body: The white marble Victoria Memorial is Kolkata's signature landmark, set in gardens at the heart of the colonial city. Nearby stand St Paul's Cathedral and the grand buildings of the old British capital.

items.title: Howrah Bridge & the River
items.body: The mighty cantilever Howrah Bridge spans the Hooghly river, alive with ferries, flower sellers and dawn bathers. The riverside ghats and the Mullick Ghat flower market are pure Kolkata.

items.title: Dakshineswar, Sweets & Street Life
items.body: Visit the Dakshineswar Kali Temple by the river, then dive into the city's famous sweets, from rasgulla to mishti doi, and its endlessly photogenic street life.

days.title: Arrive Kolkata
days.description: Met at Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free around Park Street, the city's dining heart.
suggestedActivities: Evening walk in the colonial core

days.title: Full Day Kolkata Heritage
days.description: A full day with your private vehicle covering the city's landmarks, river and markets.
suggestedActivities: Victoria Memorial and St Paul's Cathedral

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. A final round of shopping or sweets before your airport transfer.

whyBook.heading: East India, Threaded Together
whyBook.body: Kolkata is a city of layers and a good guide brings its history alive, from the colonial buildings to the artisan quarters and the food. Dhesu has arranged India journeys for over 35 years.

sections.heading: Kolkata Heritage Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Kolkata is a city for travellers who love history, culture and food over checklists. The colonial architecture, the river life and the Bengali intellectual tradition give it a character that lingers long after you leave.

This private package lets you cover the Victoria Memorial, Howrah Bridge, the temples and the markets at your own pace, with a guide who knows the city's stories and its best sweet shops.

Kolkata is also the gateway to East India and the eastern Himalayas, including Sikkim and Darjeeling.

sections.heading: What to Eat in Kolkata
sections.body:
Bengali food is a revelation. Fish curries, the street-food classic kathi roll, puchka (the local pani puri), and an entire universe of sweets built around chhena (fresh cheese).

Rasgulla, sandesh and mishti doi are non-negotiable. Your guide will point you to the century-old sweet shops where Bengalis themselves queue up.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (CCU). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Kolkata?
items.answer: October to March is the most pleasant, with cool, dry weather. The Durga Puja festival (usually October) transforms the city into a spectacular open-air art event, but it is also the busiest time. Summers are hot and humid, and the monsoon brings heavy rain.

items.question: Is Kolkata easy to get around?
items.answer: With a private vehicle and driver guide, yes. Kolkata traffic can be slow, so having a dedicated car saves time and stress, and your guide knows how to time visits to the major sights.

items.question: Can I extend to Darjeeling or Sikkim?
items.answer: Yes. Kolkata is the natural gateway to the eastern Himalayas. Sikkim, with its Kanchenjunga views and monasteries, is covered in our 7 day Sikkim package, often combined with a Kolkata start.
